contents: Require Import Types.Sigma Types.Forall Types.Arrow Types.Paths.

(** * Equivalences *)

(** ** [IsEquiv f] is logically equivalent to [IsTruncMap (-2) f] *)

Instance contr_map_isequiv {A B} (f : A -> B) `{IsEquiv _ _ f}
  : IsTruncMap (-2) f.

goals.conclusion: IsTruncMap (-2) f
IsTruncMap (-2) f

contents: intros b; refine (contr_equiv' {a : A & a = f^-1 b} _).

goals.conclusion: {a : A & a = f^-1 b} <~> hfiber f b
a = f^-1 b <~> f a = b

contents:
Definition isequiv_contr_map {A B} (f : A -> B) `{IsTruncMap (-2) A B f}
  : IsEquiv f.

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv f
IsEquiv f
B -> A
f o ?equiv_inv == idmap
?equiv_inv o f == idmap
forall x : A, ?eisretr (f x) = ap f (?eissect x)
B -> A

contents: intros b; exact (center {a : A & f a = b}).1.

goals.conclusion: f o (fun b : B => (center {a : A & f a = b}).1) == idmap
f (center {a : A & f a = b}).1 = b
(fun b : B => (center {a : A & f a = b}).1) o f == idmap
(center {a0 : A & f a0 = f a}).1 = a

contents: exact (@contr {x : A & f x = f a} _ (a;1))..1.

goals.conclusion:
forall x : A,
((fun b : B => (center {a : A & f a = b}).2)
 :
 f o (fun b : B => (center {a : A & f a = b}).1) == idmap) 
  (f x) =
ap f
  (((fun a : A => (contr (a; 1)) ..1)
    :
    (fun b : B => (center {a : A & f a = b}).1) o f == idmap) x)
(center {a0 : A & f a0 = f a}).2 = ap f (contr (a; 1)) ..1
(ap f (contr (a; 1)) ..1)^ @ (center {a0 : A & f a0 = f a}).2 = 1

transport (fun x : B => x = f a) (ap f (contr (a; 1)) ..1)
  (center {a0 : A & f a0 = f a}).2 =
1

transport (fun x : A => f x = f a) (contr (a; 1)) ..1
  (center {a0 : A & f a0 = f a}).2 =
1

contents: exact ((@contr {x : A & f x = f a} _ (a;1))..2).
(** As usual, we can't make both of these [Instances]. *)
(** It follows that when proving a map is an equivalence, we may assume its codomain is inhabited. *)

Definition isequiv_inhab_codomain {A B} (f : A -> B) (feq : B -> IsEquiv f)
  : IsEquiv f.

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv f
IsEquiv f
IsTruncMap (-2) f
Contr (hfiber f b)

contents: (** If [functor_sigma idmap g] is an equivalence then so is g. *)

goals.conclusion: forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: IsTruncMap (-2) (g a)

hypotheses.type: forall a0 : A, P a0 -> Q a0

goals.conclusion: IsTruncMap (-2) (functor_sigma idmap g)

hypotheses.type: forall a0 : A, P a0 -> Q a0

contents: (** Theorem 4.7.7: [functor_sigma idmap g] is an equivalence if and only if g is. *)

Definition equiv_total_iff_equiv_fiberwise {A} (P Q : A -> Type)
  (g : forall a, P a -> Q a)
  : IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g) <-> forall a, IsEquiv (g a).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g) <-> (forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a))

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g) <-> (forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a))

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g) -> forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: (forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a)) -> IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g) -> forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: (forall a : A, IsEquiv (g a)) -> IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (functor_sigma idmap g)

hypotheses.type: forall a : A, P a -> Q a

contents: (** ** Assuming [Funext], [IsEquiv f] is an hprop, which has further consequences *)

#[export] Instance contr_sect_equiv {A B} (f : A -> B) `{IsEquiv A B f}
    : Contr {g : B -> A & f o g == idmap}.

goals.conclusion: Contr {g : B -> A & f o g == idmap}
Contr {g : B -> A & f o g == idmap}
Contr {g : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (g x)) == idmap}

contents: refine (contr_equiv' { g : B -> A & f o g = idmap } _).

goals.conclusion:
{g : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (g x)) = idmap} <~>
{g : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (g x)) == idmap}

contents: (* Typeclass inference finds this contractible instance: it's the fiber over [idmap] of postcomposition with [f], and the latter is an equivalence since [f] is. *)

goals.conclusion: (fun x : B => f (g x)) = idmap <~> (fun x : B => f (g x)) == idmap

contents:
#[export] Instance contr_retr_equiv {A B} (f : A -> B) `{IsEquiv A B f}
    : Contr {g : B -> A & g o f == idmap}.

goals.conclusion: Contr {g : B -> A & g o f == idmap}
Contr {g : B -> A & g o f == idmap}
Contr {g : B -> A & (fun x : A => g (f x)) == idmap}

contents: refine (contr_equiv' { g : B -> A & g o f = idmap } _).

goals.conclusion:
{g : B -> A & (fun x : A => g (f x)) = idmap} <~>
{g : B -> A & (fun x : A => g (f x)) == idmap}
(fun x : A => g (f x)) = idmap <~> (fun x : A => g (f x)) == idmap

contents:
#[export] Instance hprop_isequiv {A B} (f : A -> B)
    : IsHProp (IsEquiv f).

goals.conclusion: IsHProp (IsEquiv f)
IsHProp (IsEquiv f)

contents: (* We will show that assuming [f] is an equivalence, [IsEquiv f] decomposes into a sigma of two contractible types. *)

goals.conclusion: Contr (IsEquiv f)

Contr
  {ap : {a : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (a x)) == idmap} &
  {s : (fun x : A => ap.1 (f x)) == idmap &
  forall x : A, ap.2 (f x) = Overture.ap f (s x)}}
Contr {a : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (a x)) == idmap}

Contr
  ((fun x : {a : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (a x)) == idmap} =>
    {s : (fun x0 : A => x.1 (f x0)) == idmap &
    forall x0 : A, x.2 (f x0) = ap f (s x0)})
     (center {a : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (a x)) == idmap}))

contents: (* Each of these types is equivalent to a based homotopy space.  The first is exactly [contr_sect_equiv]. *)

goals.conclusion:
Contr
  ((fun x : {a : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (a x)) == idmap} =>
    {s : (fun x0 : A => x.1 (f x0)) == idmap &
    forall x0 : A, x.2 (f x0) = ap f (s x0)})
     (center {a : B -> A & (fun x : B => f (a x)) == idmap}))

contents: (* The second requires a bit more work. *)
refine (contr_equiv' { s : f^-1 o f == idmap & eissect f == s } _).

goals.conclusion: eissect f == s <~> (forall x : A, eisretr f (f x) = ap f (s x))
eissect f a = s a <~> eisretr f (f a) = ap f (s a)
eissect f a = s a <~> ap f (eissect f a) = ap f (s a)

contents: (** Now since [IsEquiv f] and the assertion that its fibers are contractible are both HProps, logical equivalence implies equivalence. *)

Definition equiv_contr_map_isequiv {A B} (f : A -> B)
    : IsTruncMap (-2) f <~> IsEquiv f.

goals.conclusion: IsTruncMap (-2) f <~> IsEquiv f
IsTruncMap (-2) f <~> IsEquiv f

contents: (** Both directions are found by typeclass inference! *)
(** It also follows that paths of equivalences are equivalent to paths of functions. *)

Lemma equiv_path_equiv {A B : Type} (e1 e2 : A <~> B)
    : (e1 = e2 :> (A -> B)) <~> (e1 = e2 :> (A <~> B)).
equiv_via ((issig_equiv A B) ^-1 e1 = (issig_equiv A B) ^-1 e2).

goals.conclusion: e1 = e2 <~> (issig_equiv A B)^-1 e1 = (issig_equiv A B)^-1 e2
(issig_equiv A B)^-1 e1 = (issig_equiv A B)^-1 e2 <~> e1 = e2
e1 = e2 <~> (issig_equiv A B)^-1 e1 = (issig_equiv A B)^-1 e2

contents: exact (equiv_path_sigma_hprop ((issig_equiv A B)^-1 e1) ((issig_equiv A B)^-1 e2)).

#[export] Instance isequiv_path_equiv {A B : Type} {e1 e2 : A <~> B}
    : IsEquiv (@path_equiv _ _ e1 e2)
    (* Coq can find this instance by itself, but it's slow. *)
    := equiv_isequiv (equiv_path_equiv e1 e2).
(** The inverse equivalence is homotopic to [ap equiv_fun], so that is also an equivalence. *)

#[export] Instance isequiv_ap_equiv_fun {A B : Type} (e1 e2 : A <~> B)
    : IsEquiv (ap (x:=e1) (y:=e2) (@equiv_fun A B)).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (ap equiv_fun)
IsEquiv (ap equiv_fun)
IsEquiv ?f
?f == ap equiv_fun
IsEquiv (equiv_path_equiv e1 e2)^-1%equiv
(equiv_path_equiv e1 e2)^-1%equiv == ap equiv_fun
(equiv_path_equiv e1 e2)^-1%equiv p = ap equiv_fun p

contents: exact (ap_compose (fun v => (equiv_fun v; equiv_isequiv v)) pr1 p)^.

(** This implies that types of equivalences inherit truncation.  Note that we only state the theorem for [n.+1]-truncatedness, since it is not true for contractibility: if [B] is contractible but [A] is not, then [A <~> B] is not contractible because it is not inhabited.

   Don't confuse this lemma with [trunc_equiv], which says that if [A] is truncated and [A] is equivalent to [B], then [B] is truncated.  It would be nice to find a better pair of names for them. *)

#[export] Instance istrunc_equiv {n : trunc_index} {A B : Type} `{IsTrunc n.+1 B}
    : IsTrunc n.+1 (A <~> B).

goals.conclusion: IsTrunc n.+1 (A <~> B)
IsTrunc n.+1 (A <~> B)
forall x y : A <~> B, IsTrunc n (x = y)
IsTrunc n (e1 = e2)

contents: (** In the contractible case, we have to assume that *both* types are contractible to get a contractible type of equivalences. *)

#[export] Instance contr_equiv_contr_contr {A B : Type} `{Contr A} `{Contr B}
    : Contr (A <~> B).

goals.conclusion: Contr (A <~> B)
Contr (A <~> B)
forall y : A <~> B, equiv_contr_contr = y
equiv_contr_contr = e
equiv_contr_contr == e

contents: (** The type of *automorphisms* of an hprop is always contractible *)

#[export] Instance contr_aut_hprop A `{IsHProp A}
    : Contr (A <~> A).

goals.conclusion: Contr (A <~> A)
Contr (A <~> A)
forall y : A <~> A, 1%equiv = y
1%equiv == e

contents:
Definition functor_equiv {A B C D} (h : A <~> C) (k : B <~> D)
    : (A <~> B) -> (C <~> D)
    := fun f => ((k oE f) oE h^-1).

#[export] Instance isequiv_functor_equiv {A B C D} (h : A <~> C) (k : B <~> D)
    : IsEquiv (functor_equiv h k).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv (functor_equiv h k)
IsEquiv (functor_equiv h k)
(fun x : C <~> D => functor_equiv h k (functor_equiv h^-1 k^-1 x)) == idmap
(fun x : A <~> B => functor_equiv h^-1 k^-1 (functor_equiv h k x)) == idmap
(fun x : C <~> D => functor_equiv h k (functor_equiv h^-1 k^-1 x)) == idmap

contents: intros f; apply path_equiv, path_arrow; intros x; simpl.
exact (eisretr k _ @ ap f (eisretr h x)).

goals.conclusion: (fun x : A <~> B => functor_equiv h^-1 k^-1 (functor_equiv h k x)) == idmap

contents: intros g; apply path_equiv, path_arrow; intros x; simpl.
exact (eissect k _ @ ap g (eissect h x)).

Definition equiv_functor_equiv {A B C D} (h : A <~> C) (k : B <~> D)
    : (A <~> B) <~> (C <~> D)
    := Build_Equiv _ _ (functor_equiv h k) _.

Definition equiv_functor_precompose_equiv@{i j k u v | i <= u, j <= v, k <= u, k <= v}
    {X : Type@{i}} {Y : Type@{j}} (Z : Type@{k}) (e : X <~> Y)
    : Equiv@{v u} (Y <~> Z) (X <~> Z)
    := equiv_functor_equiv e^-1%equiv 1%equiv.

Definition equiv_functor_postcompose_equiv@{i j k u v | i <= u, j <= v, k <= u, k <= v}
    {X : Type@{i}} {Y : Type@{j}} (Z : Type@{k}) (e : X <~> Y)
    : Equiv@{u v} (Z <~> X) (Z <~> Y)
    := equiv_functor_equiv 1%equiv e.

#[export]  Instance isequiv_equiv_inverse {A B}
    : IsEquiv (@equiv_inverse A B).

goals.conclusion: IsEquiv equiv_inverse
IsEquiv equiv_inverse

contents:
refine (isequiv_adjointify _ equiv_inverse _ _);
      intros e; apply path_equiv; reflexivity.

Definition equiv_equiv_inverse A B
    : (A <~> B) <~> (B <~> A)
    := Build_Equiv _ _ (@equiv_inverse A B) _.

(** ** Other facts about equivalences *)

(** This is like [transport_arrow], but for a family of equivalence types. It just shows that the functions are homotopic. *)

goals.conclusion:
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f y =
transport C p (f (transport B p^ y))

transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f y =
transport C p (f (transport B p^ y))

contents: (** A version that shows that the underlying functions are equal. *)

goals.conclusion: transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f = transport C p o f o transport B p^
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f = transport C p o f o transport B p^

contents: (** A version that shows that the equivalences are equal.  Here we do need [Funext], for [path_equiv]. *)

goals.conclusion:
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f =
equiv_transport C p oE f oE equiv_transport B p^

transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f =
equiv_transport C p oE f oE equiv_transport B p^

transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C x) p f =
equiv_transport C p oE f oE equiv_transport B p^

contents: (** The special case when the codomain type is constant. *)

goals.conclusion: transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f y = f (transport B p^ y)
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f y = f (transport B p^ y)
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f = f o transport B p^
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f = f o transport B p^
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f = f oE equiv_transport B p^
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f = f oE equiv_transport B p^
transport (fun x : A => B x <~> C) p f = f oE equiv_transport B p^

contents: (** The special case when the domain type is constant. *)

goals.conclusion: transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f y = transport C p (f y)
transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f y = transport C p (f y)
transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f = transport C p o f
transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f = transport C p o f
transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f = equiv_transport C p oE f
transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f = equiv_transport C p oE f
transport (fun x : A => B <~> C x) p f = equiv_transport C p oE f
